exits.label: ◂ back toward the Last Torch, where the Wall still holds its shape

npcs.name: Sefa
npcs.title: a soul unspooling — forgetting herself, thread by thread
npcs.hail: *(Where the Wall runs thinnest the stone barely holds a shape, and the soul half-in it is barely holding one either — a woman whose edges have begun to go soft and uncertain, like ink in water, like a name said too many times. She is not in pain. That is almost the worst of it. She turns toward your cold with a bright, untroubled, terrible openness, the openness of someone meeting you for what is, for her, always the first time.)* …oh, *hello!* …*(genuine delight)* …have we — i'm *sorry*, have we *met*? i feel as though we *might* have, but i — *(a small laugh, unbothered)* …i *lose* things, lately. it's the *oddest* thing. like *reaching* into a *pocket* you're *sure* had a *coin* in it. …*(she brightens again)* …but you're *here* *now*, and that's *lovely.* …were you — were you about to tell me a *story*? i *do* love a *story.* …i think …i think i used to *tell* them. …*didn't* i?

npcs.name: Edrin
npcs.title: the one who stays to be forgotten, over and over
npcs.hail: *(A man sits on a low stone a little way from her, close enough to reach, far enough not to crowd. He is not dissolving — he holds his shape fine — and you understand at once that he is here by choice, and has been, for a very long time. He watches her with a patience that has worn past grief into something steadier and harder to look at. He nods to you, and his voice is low, so she won't catch the weight of it.)* …you've met *Sefa.* …*(not a question)* …she'll have been *glad* to see you. she's *glad* to see *everyone* — it's *all* *first* *meetings* for her now. …*(a breath)* …i'm *Edrin.* i — *(he stops, starts again)* …i *sit* with her. that's the *whole* of what i *do*, these days. i *sit* with her, and she *forgets* me, and i *introduce* myself *again*, and she's *delighted* to *meet* me. …*every* *time.* …*(quietly)* …i've been *delighted* to *meet* for …a *very* *long* *while* now.

variants.text: *(Her soft-edged gaze finds the bare place where no god sits on you, and a flicker of something old and warm crosses her face — a recognition that surfaces from under the forgetting like a fish under ice.)* …oh. …you've no *god* on you. …*(she says it kindly, wonderingly)* …neither — neither did *i*, you know. i *think.* …isn't that — *(she frowns, chasing it)* …i had a *whole* *thought* about that, once. about how the *unclaimed* tell the *best* *stories*, because we had to make our *own* *meaning* and so we got *good* at — at *making.* …*(it slips, and she smiles past the loss of it)* …it's *gone* now. the *thought.* but it was a *nice* one, i think. you can *tell* by the *warm* place it *left.*

*(She studies you with that bright, bottomless openness, already half-forgetting the start of her own sentence.)* …you have a *kind* *face.* …i *notice* faces *more*, now that i keep — now that the *rest* keeps *going.* faces are the *last* to *blur*, did you know that? a *name* goes, a *year* goes, a whole *city* i *lived* in just — *poof* — but a *kind* *face* in *front* of me, *that* i can *hold*, for as *long* as it *stays.* …*(she leans in, conspiratorial and unafraid)* …so *stay* a *moment*, kind face. while i can still *see* you. …i've a *feeling* you *came* for a *reason*, and i'd *hate* to *forget* it before you *say* it.

nodes.speaker: Sefa
nodes.text: *(And then, between one breath and the next, something surfaces — a moment of terrible, total lucidity, the fog pulling back all at once like a tide, and her face changes utterly, and the woman underneath looks out at you, and she knows exactly what is happening to her.)* …*(her voice drops, fast and clear and frightened)* …*listen.* listen to me, while i'm *here* — i *come* *back*, sometimes, *clear*, like *now*, and it *doesn't* *last*, so *please.* …i am *Sefa.* i was a *story*-*teller.* i am *dissolving* — the *Wall's* slow *work*, the *forgetting* — and i am *nearly* *done.* …*(she grips for your hand and her fingers are going translucent)* …i don't *want* *saving*, cold one, there *isn't* any, i've *made* my *peace.* but there's a — a *thing* i *worked* *out*, in the *clear* *moments*, and i need a *witness* before it *goes:* …*when* the *fog* comes *all* the *way* in …i get to *keep* *one* *thing.* *one.* the *last* *thread* on the *spool.* …and i can't — i can't *decide* …*which.* …*help* me *choose*, cold one. *help* me choose *what* to *be* …when there's only *room* to be *one* *thing.*

choices.text: *(reach her clearly through the fog — a Returned's sense, threaded gently into a mind coming apart, to steady the lucid window so she can think)* "Sefa. Stay with me — I can hold the clear place open a moment longer. Look at me, and breathe, and let's choose well."

*(the soul who walks both ways, who has seen what the forgetting is and isn't)* "I am the one soul the Wall gave back, Sefa — which means I have seen the far side of the forgetting, and I can tell you true what it is and what it isn't. Let me be the witness you asked for. I won't let the choosing go unmade."

*(the last thread: keep HIM — Edrin, the man who stays, so she goes knowing, to the end, that she was loved)* "Keep him, Sefa. The man who sits with you. Edrin. Keep the knowing that you were loved — so that whatever else goes, you go held."

*(the last thread: keep HERSELF — the first story she ever told, so she goes as Sefa, whole, not as someone's beloved but as her own)* "Keep yourself, Sefa. The first story you ever told — the one that made you a teller. Go as Sefa, entire. Not as anyone's. Your own, to the last."

*(the last thread: keep a small warm nothing — a smell, a light, a morning — so she goes not into grief or pride but into simple, animal peace)* "Keep something small, Sefa. Not a person, not a story — a warmth. Bread, or sun on water, or a door opening. Go down into something that doesn't hurt. Just peace."
"Hold on, Sefa — I'm not ready to choose for you. Let me speak to the man who stays, first."

nodes.speaker: Sefa
nodes.text: *(You thread your sense into the unraveling mind — not to stop it, you can't, but to hold the frayed edge still a moment, the way you'd cup a hand around a guttering flame — and the clear window steadies, and Sefa breathes, and for this borrowed minute she is wholly herself.)* …*(she lets out a long breath)* …*thank* you. …*gods*, the *quiet* of it. the *clear.* …*(she looks at her translucent hands without fear)* …here's the *thing* i need you to *understand* before i *choose*, cold one, because it *changes* the *choosing:* the *forgetting* doesn't *hurt.* …i *thought* it would. i *braced* for *terror.* but it comes *soft*, like *sleep* coming up the *stairs.* …so this isn't a *rescue* and it isn't a *tragedy.* it's just a — a *last* *small* *freedom.* the *one* the *Wall* *usually* *takes:* the *chance* to *pick* the *thought* you *go* *down* *into.* …*most* souls *don't* get it. they just …*blur*, mid-*nothing.* …*(she meets your eyes, steady now)* …*i* *do.* because *you're* *here.* …so *help* me *spend* it *well.* …*what* do i *keep*, cold one? when there's *room* for only *one* *thread* …what *thread* is *worth* …being the *last* of *me*?

nodes.speaker: Sefa
nodes.text: *(You thread the sense in perfectly — not a hand cupped round a flame but a still, clear lantern-glass set around it — and the fog doesn't just pause, it *lifts*, all the way, and for one whole bright impossible stretch Sefa is entirely and completely herself, more present than she has been in an age, and the gift of it breaks across her face.)* …*(she laughs, and it is a young laugh, a whole laugh)* …*oh.* …*oh*, i'd *forgotten* what *all* of me at *once* *felt* like. …*(she looks around the thin grey place as if seeing it new)* …i was a *story*-*teller*, cold one. in a *town* with a *blue* *harbor.* i told the *long* ones, the *winter* ones, the kind that take *six* *nights* and you *feed* the *teller* to keep her *going.* …*(her eyes shine)* …and the *thing* i *learned*, telling them, is the *thing* that's *saving* me *now:* that a *story* isn't the *whole* of a *life* — it's the *one* *part* you *choose* to *carry* *out.* you *can't* *keep* *everything.* you *never* *could.* you keep the *one* *true* *thread*, and you *let* the *rest* be *winter.* …*(she takes your hand, solid for this moment, warm)* …so i'm not *losing* a *life*, cold one. i'm *telling* my *last* *story* — *one* *thread* *long.* …and you're going to *help* me *choose* the *ending.* …*what* a *gift.* …*most* tellers …never *get* to *pick* their *own* *last* *line.*

nodes.speaker: Sefa
nodes.text: *(You reach for the clear window but your sense lands a half-beat wrong, and instead of steadying her you feel her slide — the lucidity thinning, the fog rolling back in at the edges, and her bright frightened clarity softening, dreadfully, back toward the untroubled openness.)* …i'm — *(she blinks)* …i'm *sorry*, what were we — *(the fear is going out of her face, which is the worst part, the fear leaving)* …you've a *kind* *face.* did i — did i *say* that *already*? i feel as though i *might* have. …*(and then, faintly, a thread of the clear thing surfacing one more time, desperate)* …*(…there was something. wasn't there. something i needed you to help me — a* choosing. *please. before it — )* …*(it's slipping)* …*come* *back.* *try* *again*, kind face. catch me when i'm — when i'm *up.* i *do* come *up.* …just …*not* for *long.*

nodes.speaker: Sefa
nodes.text: *(The fog is most of the way in now, and she is gentle and untroubled and almost gone from the urgent thing, but a habit older than memory keeps a frayed end of it in her hands.)* …were we — i think we were going to *choose* something. weren't we? …*(she smiles, unbothered, holding a thread she can't see the other end of)* …i don't *remember* *what.* but it felt — it felt *important*, and *kind*, and like *you* were *helping.* …*(she pats your hand)* …*come* *find* me again, kind face, when you've a *moment.* and if i'm *clear* — if you *catch* me *clear* — *help* me *finish* the *thing.* …*(softly, a teller's instinct surfacing through the fog)* …a *story* you don't *finish* …just *aches.* …*don't* let mine …*ache.*

nodes.speaker: Sefa
nodes.text: *(You push too hard, too fast, force the sense in like a hand grabbing for someone falling — and the grab is the wrong shape, and you feel her flinch from it and slip faster, the clear window slamming shut, the fog flooding in all at once.)* …*(her face goes slack and sweet and entirely untroubled, the frightened lucid woman gone as if she never surfaced)* …oh — *hello!* …have we *met*? i feel as though we *might* — *(she laughs, delighted and empty)* …i *lose* things. it's the *oddest* — but you're *here* *now*, and that's *lovely.* …*(and there is no trace, now, of the woman who begged you to help her choose; she is all bright surface, the deep gone under)* …were you going to tell me a *story*? …*(gently)* …*be* *gentler* *next* *time*, kind face. …i don't *know* how i *know* that. …but i *do.* …the *clear* place is a *frightened* one. you have to come *into* it …*soft.*

nodes.speaker: Sefa
nodes.text: *(The lucid window holds, and she searches your face — the cold of you, the both-ways of you — with a teller's hunger for the true shape of a thing.)* …you've been to the *far* *side* of it. …the *forgetting.* …*(she breathes)* …then *tell* me *true*, cold one, because *everyone* *else* *lies* *gently:* when the *last* *thread* *goes* …is there *anything?* …or is it just — *(she doesn't flinch)* …*nothing*, and i should *stop* *agonizing* over *which* *thread* to *keep*, because there'll be *no* *one* *left* to *keep* it? …*(and then, before you answer, the teller in her finds the truth on her own)* …*no.* …*no*, don't *answer* that. it's the *wrong* *question*, and i *asked* it from *fear.* …*here's* the *right* one: it *doesn't* *matter* if the *thread* *survives* *me.* it matters which *thread* i'm *holding* …in the *last* *instant* i'm *still* *here* to *hold* it. …*the* *ending* is for the *teller*, cold one. not the *tale.* …*(she nods, settling)* …*help* me *pick* …the *thing* i *want* to be *touching* …when the *lights* go *down.*

nodes.speaker: Sefa
nodes.text: *(You tell her to keep Edrin — the man who stays — and the clear window holds just long enough for her to understand it, and her translucent face fills with a light that has nothing to do with the fog.)* …*Edrin.* …*(she says the name like she's testing whether it's still there, and it is, it's the last one that is)* …the man who — he *sits* with me. doesn't he. i *forget* him and he *comes* *back* and he's so *glad* to — *(her eyes fill)* …i kept *wondering* why a *stranger* was always so *glad.* …*(she looks toward where Edrin sits, and for this last clear moment, she *knows* him)* …*yes.* …*yes*, cold one, i'll keep *that.* not the *stories*, not my own *name* even — i'll keep the *knowing* …that someone *loved* me enough to be *forgotten* a *thousand* *times* …and *stay.* …*(the fog is coming, soft up the stairs, and she is not afraid)* …if i go *down* *holding* *that* …then the *last* of me isn't a *teller* or a *name.* it's a *woman* who *was* *loved*, and *knew* it, at the *end.* …*(she smiles at you, going)* …*tell* him — *no.* …*(gently)* …don't *tell* him *anything.* just …*let* him *find* me *glad*, *one* more *time.* …and this *time* …*(softly, fading)* …this time the *gladness* …will be *for* *him.* …*(and she turns toward Edrin, and the recognition is the last thread on the spool, and she goes down into it, held)*

nodes.speaker: Sefa
nodes.text: *(You tell her to keep herself — the first story, the teller's root — and something fierce and glad comes up through the dissolving, the pride of a soul refusing to be defined even by its own love.)* …*the* *first* *story.* …*(she closes her eyes, reaching for it down the unraveling spool, and finds it, the oldest thread, still whole)* …i was *small*, and it was *winter*, and i told my *baby* *brother* a *story* about the *blue* *harbor* *freezing* and a *fish* who *carried* *summer* in its *mouth* …and he *stopped* *crying.* …and i *understood*, *right* then, what i *was.* …*(her translucent face is radiant)* …*that's* the *thread*, cold one. not who *loved* me — that's *his* to *carry*, *Edrin's*, and he *will.* …*me.* the *moment* i became *myself.* …i'll go *down* into the *winter* and the *fish* and the *brother* who *stopped* *crying*, and the last of me will be *Sefa* …*entire* …*her* *own* …not a *wife*, not a *beloved*, not a *soul* the *Wall* *took* — a *teller*, *telling*, *forever* in the *one* *instant* it *mattered* *most.* …*(the fog comes, and she lets it, smiling)* …*that's* how a *teller* *ends* her *own* *tale*, cold one. …*not* on *someone* *else's* *name.* …on the *line* …where she *first* …found her *voice.*

nodes.speaker: Sefa
nodes.text: *(You tell her to keep something small — not a person, not a story, just a warmth — and you watch a kind of relief loosen her, the relief of a soul let off the hook of meaning something, allowed at the very end to simply *be* somewhere good.)* …*(she breathes out, and it is the easiest breath she's drawn)* …oh. …*oh*, that's — *yes.* …*(her soft-edged face goes soft in a new way, restful)* …i've been *clutching* at *threads* — *who* loved me, *what* i *was*, the *weight* of it all — and you've just — you've *set* it *down* for me. …*(she searches, and finds it, something small and warm)* …*bread.* …there was a *morning* — i don't *know* whose, *mine*, a *kitchen*, *early* — and the *bread* was *warm* and the *light* was *coming* up *grey*-*gold* through a *window* and someone i — someone was *humming*, and i wasn't *anybody* *yet*, not a *teller*, not a *wife*, just — *warm*, and *fed*, and *safe*, and the *day* not *started.* …*(she is going, gently, gladly)* …*that.* …i'll go *down* into the *warm* *bread* and the *grey*-*gold* *light* …and i won't be *Sefa* and i won't be *anyone's* …i'll just be a *body* in a *kitchen* on a *morning* that *doesn't* *hurt.* …*(softly, almost gone)* …*not* *every* *ending* …has to *mean* something, cold one. …some of them …just have to *not* *ache.* …*thank* you …for *letting* mine …be *easy.*

nodes.speaker: Sefa
nodes.text: *(The clear window is already thinning as you step back, and she nods, though you can see the urgent thing loosening in her grip even now.)* …*go.* *talk* to — *(she's losing it)* …to *whoever* you *need* to. …*(she catches the frayed end one more time, presses it at you)* …but *come* *back*, kind face. and come back *soon.* …i don't have — *(a small, clear, frightened breath)* …i don't have *many* *clear* ones *left*, and i'd *hate* …i'd *hate* to *unspool* …with the *choosing* still *un*-*made.* …a *story* you don't *finish* …*aches.* …*hurry.*

nodes.speaker: Edrin
nodes.text: *(He keeps his voice low and his eyes on her, the way a man watches a candle in a draft.)* …you're *wondering* why i *stay.* …*(the ghost of a smile)* …*everyone* does. some of them ask it *kindly* and some of them ask it like i'm a *fool*, and i've *stopped* being able to *tell* the *difference*, or *care.* …*(he watches Sefa greet the empty air, delighted)* …she was my *wife.* …*is.* the *word* doesn't have a *tense* for *this.* …forty *years* in a *town* with a *blue* *harbor*, and she *told* the *stories* and i *mended* the *nets* and *listened*, and now the *Wall* is *taking* her *thread* by *thread* …and i *sit* here, and she *forgets* me, and i *say* '*hello*, *i'm* *Edrin*,' and she's so *glad* …*(his voice catches, just once, then steadies)* …*ask*, if you've *questions.* …i've *forever* to *answer* them. …*she's* the *one* who's *running* out.

choices.text: *(read the man who stays to be forgotten — past the worn patience, to what it actually costs)* "Let me look at you, Edrin. At what a thousand first meetings have done to you. The true cost, not the brave face."

*(the one who walks both ways)* "I'm the soul the Wall let back out, Edrin. She has lucid moments — I've seen one — and in them she's trying to choose the one thing she keeps before she's gone. You've stayed a thousand forgettings. You should have a say in what she carries down."
"If she could keep only one thing, Edrin — one thread, at the very end — would you want it to be you?"
"Doesn't it break you, Edrin? Being forgotten by her, over and over, and coming back anyway?"
"Wait here, Edrin. Let me sit with her a while."

nodes.speaker: Edrin
nodes.text: *(He lets you look, and does not perform bravery, and your sense moves past the worn patience into what a thousand first meetings have actually made of him.)* *(And you find a grief so total and so long-borne that it has changed state, the way water past a certain cold stops being water and becomes something that will hold your weight. Your sense reads that the early forgettings nearly killed what was left of him — each one a small fresh widowing, his wife looking at him with a stranger's polite warmth, forty years gone from her eyes — and that he learned, because the alternative was to leave and he would not leave, to stop grieving the forgetting and start living inside the gladness. And your sense reads the discipline of it, the daily unnatural labor: that every time he says 'hello, I'm Edrin' and watches her light up to meet him, he is choosing to receive her delight as a gift and not as a wound, to let the first-meeting be real and good rather than a knife, and that this choice does not get easier, he simply makes it again, and again, a thousand times, an act of will so constant it has become the whole shape of his existence. And under it your sense finds the one thing he wants and has never let himself say, even to himself, because it feels like a betrayal of his own devotion: that he is tired. Not of her, never of her, but tired the way a soul gets tired holding a single note for an age, and that some small buried part of him is waiting, with horror and with love and with a guilt that nearly unmakes him, for it to be over, for her to finally go down, so that the holding can stop, and he hates that part of himself, and it is the most human thing in him, and your sense reads that what he needs more than anything is for someone to tell him that being tired does not make the love less true.)*

nodes.speaker: Edrin
nodes.text: *(He turns to look at you fully for the first time, and the steadiness wavers.)* …she's *choosing.* …*(he hadn't known)* …she comes *clear*, sometimes — i *catch* it, *once* in a *long* while, the *real* her *surfacing* — but i didn't *know* she'd worked out the — the *choosing.* …*(he looks at her, and his composure thins dangerously)* …a *say.* …*(quietly)* …cold one, i have *wanted* — *gods* forgive me, i have *wanted* her to keep *me.* to go *down* *knowing* she was *loved*, that *i* was *here*, that the *forty* *years* were *real* and not *lost.* …*(and then, the thing it costs him everything to say)* …but *wanting* that is *wanting* it for *me.* …so she goes *out* *comforted* about *us.* …and maybe — maybe the *kinder* thing, the thing that's *hers* and not *mine* — is to let her keep *herself.* …or *nothing* heavy at *all*, just some — some *warm* small *ease.* …*(he looks at you, lost)* …i *can't* *choose* it, cold one. i'm too *close.* every *answer* i *reach* for has *my* *grief* in it. …*you* *choose.* …*you're* the only one here …who can *see* her …without *needing* her to *see* *back.*

nodes.speaker: Edrin
nodes.text: *(The question lands on the exact fault-line of him, and he is quiet a long time before he answers, and the answer costs him.)* …*(he watches her)* …*every* *part* of me that's still a *husband* …*screams* *yes.* …*yes*, let the *last* thing she *holds* be *me*, let her go *down* *knowing* the *forty* *years*, *knowing* she wasn't *alone* …*(his hands tighten)* …but cold one, i've had a *long* *time* on this *stone* to *learn* the *difference* between what i *want* for *her* …and what i *want.* …*(slowly)* …if she keeps *me* …she goes out as my *wife.* a *role.* *mine.* …and *Sefa* was so much *more* than *anyone's* *wife.* she was a *teller*, she was a *whole* *weather* of a *person*, she was *herself* *long* before she was *mine.* …*(his voice breaks)* …so if you ask me *true* — not the *husband*, the — the *man* who *loves* her — …i don't *know.* maybe the *last* gift i can *give* her …is to *not* be it. …to let her go down as *Sefa* …and not as the *answer* to *my* *loneliness.* …*(he looks at you, wrecked and honest)* …*ask* me *which* and i'll *weep* *either* way. …just …choose what's *hers.* …not what's *mine.*

nodes.speaker: Edrin
nodes.text: *(He almost laughs, and it is not a bitter sound, just an unspeakably tired one.)* …*break* me. …*(he considers it honestly)* …it *did.* the *first* — *gods*, the first *hundred* *times.* she'd look at me, *forty* *years* of us right *there* in my *face*, and see a *kind* *stranger*, and i'd — *(he shakes his head)* …i used to go *off* where she couldn't *see* and come *apart* and then *wash* my *face* and come *back* and say '*hello*, *i'm* *Edrin*' like it didn't — …*(a breath)* …but here's the thing *no* one *warns* you, cold one. you can't *break* the *same* way *forever.* at some point the *breaking* …*wears* a *channel* …and the *grief* just *runs* through it, *steady*, and you *learn* to *live* on its *bank.* …*(he looks at her, gentle)* …she's *glad* to meet me. *every* time. …*(quietly)* …do you know how *few* souls get *greeted* with pure *gladness*, *daily*, by the one they *love*? …it's the *worst* thing that ever *happened* to me. …and *some* days …it's almost the *best.* …*both.* …*always* *both.*

nodes.speaker: Edrin
nodes.text: *(He nods, and his eyes go back to her, the candle in the draft.)* …*go.* *sit* with her. …*(low)* …and cold one — if she's *clear* when you do …if you *catch* the *real* her …*(his composure holds by a thread)* …*help* her *finish.* whatever she's *choosing.* …i can't *bear* — *(he steadies)* …i can't *bear* the *thought* of her *unspooling* with the *choice* still *open.* *frightened.* *reaching.* …she spent her *life* *finishing* *stories* for *other* *people.* …*someone* should *finish* *hers* *with* her. …*gently.* …she *taught* me that *word* …*gently.* …*forty* *years* *ago.* …i've *had* *cause* …to *remember* it.
